title: Bridge Essay: Not all about sex: neural and biobehavioral functions of human dance.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Water/Pleasure** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Water and pleasure — flow state, peak experience, aesthetic pleasure, reward systems, pleasure neuroscience, hedonic tone, musical pleasure.

**Not all about sex: neural and biobehavioral functions of human dance.** — Ann N Y Acad Sci. PMID: 28787539. DOI: 10.1111/nyas.13420. This paper provides an integrative review of neuroscientific and biobehavioral evidence about the effects of dance on the individual across cultural differences. Dance moves us, and many derive aesthetic pleasure from it. However, in addition-and beyond aesthetics-we propose that dance has noteworthy, deeper neurobiological effects. We first summarize evidence that illustrates the centrality of dance to human life indirectly from archaeology, comparative psychology, developmental psychology, and...

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 5 (Water/Pleasure). Evaluation: Excellent (score=6.3)
